title: Segment String into Dictionary Words
description:
Given a string s and a dictionary wordDict, determine if s can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ |s| ≤ 300
- 1 ≤ |wordDict| ≤ 10^3
- 1 ≤ word length ≤ 20
explanation:
Intuition
Let dp[i] indicate whether s[:i] is segmentable. For each i, check any word length L where s[i-L:i] is in the dictionary and dp[i-L] is True.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Put dictionary in a set and track max word length
2. Main Algorithm: dp[0]=True; for i in 1..n check j from max(0,i-L)..i and probe substrings
3. Return Result: Return dp[n]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n·L)
- Space Complexity: O(n)

Key Insights
- Limit j by max word length for efficiency
- Set membership gives O(1) lookups

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting dp[0]=True blocks all segments
- Slicing too broadly increases cost
hints: Bound checks by max word length
Use a set for O(1) lookup
